USER-PREFERENCES DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side of the argument too') — counter-arguments considered before electing NO-OP:
  (a) Should I write a tracking EXP for the 8.0-day manual-commit drought threshold-cross? — REJECTED: decider already labelled this in its run mode; PRED-105 verdict is already silent_ignore; adding another EXP about the same observation would be noise. The drought is incremental, not categorical.
  (b) Should I attempt Mode 2 work despite all-items-delivered? — REJECTED: this is exactly how the EXP-255 and EXP-256/EXP-257 duplications happened twice this week. Doing 'more' on a fully-delivered HNOTE creates pipeline garbage. The HNOTE remains pending only because the decider hasn't yet flipped consumed.
  (c) Should I scan the 41 chg files for orphans? — DECLINED: changes_pending_analysis=0 is the dispatcher's authoritative signal; second-guessing it would cross into tinker territory. If genuine orphans exist, that is an integrity-pipeline finding, not an analyst finding.
  (d) Is there a 'find the kernel of truth' opportunity in chg-001 worth surfacing? — CONSIDERED: the kernel here is that the dome's Daily Review pipeline canonically holds at refined=4 even as PRED-105's window closes — i.e., the pipeline IS designed to silent-ignore unfavorable predictions, not just incidentally fail to update. This kernel is already documented in our Section 4.2.5 (PRED-105 silent-ignore) and in Section 5.16 (monitoring infrastructure analysis). No new angle to add this poll.
